name: Serge Resonant EQ (RESEQ)
description: Ten-band Serge resonant equalizer with dual inputs, two identical mix outputs, two comb outputs, and switchable positive or inverted feedback.

controls.name: Band 1-10
controls.description: Sets the ten band gains and resonance points. Noon is flat, clockwise boosts into resonance and counterclockwise cuts more deeply.
controls.detail: center: flat · >3 o'clock: resonant · <9 o'clock: rejection

controls.name: Input A
controls.description: Sets the level of the first audio input before the EQ bank.

controls.name: Input B
controls.description: Sets the level of the second audio input before the EQ bank.

controls.name: Feedback
controls.description: Sets how much of the module's output is fed back into the resonant equalizer path.

controls.name: Phase switch
controls.description: Selects normal or inverted feedback for more surgical reinforcement or cancellation.

inputs.name: IN A
inputs.description: First audio input to the internal two-channel mixer.

inputs.name: IN B
inputs.description: Second audio input to the internal two-channel mixer.

outputs.name: MIX OUT 1
outputs.description: Main equalized output, one of two identical mix outputs.

outputs.name: MIX OUT 2
outputs.description: Second identical equalized output for parallel processing.

outputs.name: COMB 1
outputs.description: Output that sums alternating filter bands for comb-style spectral shaping.

outputs.name: COMB 2
outputs.description: Second comb output with the alternate band grouping.

behaviors.name: Flat-at-noon response
behaviors.description: When the ten band knobs are at noon, the main EQ output is flat.

behaviors.name: Resonant top end
behaviors.description: Bands become noticeably resonant instead of merely boosted, which gives the module formant-like peaks.

behaviors.name: Deeper rejection
behaviors.description: The band moves into stronger attenuation rather than a simple gentle cut.

behaviors.name: Comb outputs interact strongly
behaviors.description: Both Comb outputs are highly sensitive to band frequency changes, so even small knob moves reshape the sums.

behaviors.name: Input drive and coloration
behaviors.description: The two-input mixer can push the EQ stage into color and distortion before the band bank.

behaviors.name: Feedback phase control
behaviors.trigger: use the feedback knob and phase switch
behaviors.description: Normal feedback reinforces the current spectral shape; inverted feedback produces phase cancellation and hollowed-out resonances.

behaviors.name: Serge-style band spacing
behaviors.trigger: set the bands across the panel
behaviors.description: Most bands are spaced at major-seventh intervals, with the top and bottom bands acting as end caps.

patchIdeas.name: Formant vocal sweep
patchIdeas.patch: saw VCO into IN A · raise bands around the 500 Hz to 3 kHz region · drive one comb output lightly into the mix · the equalizer acts like a fixed formant bank for synthetic vowels and spoken tones.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ResEQ:
* Band 1-10: sculpted for vowels
* Feedback: low

VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> ResEQ (IN A)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 1) -> Output (L)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 2) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Comb stereo split
patchIdeas.patch: mono source into IN A and IN B via the mixer · route COMB 1 and COMB 2 to separate channels · move the band knobs as a stereo performance control rather than a static EQ.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ResEQ:
* Input A: noon
* Input B: noon
* Feedback: off

Mixer:
Output:

- Mixer (Out) -> ResEQ (IN A)
- Mixer (Out) -> ResEQ (IN B)
- ResEQ (COMB 1) -> Output (L)
- ResEQ (COMB 2) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Resonant ping bank
patchIdeas.patch: short trigger burst into IN A through a VCA · bias the upper bands upward · the module rings like a tuned metallic object without needing a separate resonator.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ResEQ:
* Band 1-10: bright and peaked
* Feedback: medium

VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:
Trigger:
Output:

- Trigger (Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> ResEQ (IN A)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 1) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Inverted feedback growl
patchIdeas.patch: feedback knob high · phase switch inverted · saw VCO into IN A · the spectrum hollows and folds back into itself for unstable growl and reed-like overtones.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ResEQ:
* Feedback: high
* Phase switch: inverted

VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> ResEQ (IN A)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 1) -> Output (In)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 2) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Spectral crossfade
patchIdeas.patch: VCO A into IN A and VCO B into IN B · use the band knobs as a fixed spectral crossfader between two sources · COMB outputs provide additional movement while the main outputs stay usable as a consistent stereo pair.
patchIdeas.diagram:
ResEQ:
* Input A: low
* Input B: low
* Band 1-10: wide spread

VCO A:
VCO B:
Output:

- VCO A (Out) -> ResEQ (IN A)
- VCO B (Out) -> ResEQ (IN B)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 1) -> Output (L)
- ResEQ (MIX OUT 2) -> Output (R)
